rows.requirement: UCP signs and verifies HTTP messages using RFC 9421 HTTP Message Signatures across all HTTP-based transports.
rows.quote:
UCP uses HTTP Message Signatures ([RFC 9421](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9421))
for all HTTP-based transports:
rows.notes: Request-signature is STUBBED as literal "test" in the suite (integration_test_utils get_headers, L133); the suite does NOT verify signatures.

rows.requirement: All implementations MUST support verifying P-256 (ES256) signatures.
rows.quote: All implementations **MUST** support verifying P-256 (`ES256`) signatures
rows.notes: Verification row; suite stubs request-signature so official_oracle=false.

rows.requirement: Support for P-384 (ES384) signatures is OPTIONAL.
rows.quote: Support for P-384 (`ES384`) is **OPTIONAL**

rows.requirement: Signers SHOULD use P-256 for maximum compatibility.
rows.quote: Signers **SHOULD** use P-256 for maximum compatibility

rows.requirement: Signers MAY use P-384 when both parties support it.
rows.quote: Signers **MAY** use P-384 when both parties support it

rows.requirement: The algorithm is derived from the key's crv field in the JWK; alg is NOT included in Signature-Input parameters.
rows.quote:
The algorithm is derived from the key's `crv` field in the JWK;
  `alg` is **NOT** included in `Signature-Input` parameters
rows.notes: Phrased as a prohibition (alg NOT in Signature-Input).

rows.requirement: Public keys MUST be represented using JSON Web Key (JWK) format as defined in RFC 7517.
rows.quote:
Public keys **MUST** be represented using **JSON Web Key (JWK)** format as
defined in [RFC 7517](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7517).

rows.requirement: Public keys are published in the signing_keys array of the party's UCP profile at /.well-known/ucp.
rows.quote:
Public keys are published in the `signing_keys` array of the party's UCP
profile at `/.well-known/ucp`.
rows.notes: Key discovery via signing_keys[] in profile; doc-structural so browser_capable=true.

rows.requirement: During key rotation, continue accepting signatures from old keys for a grace period of minimum 7 days.
rows.quote: **Grace period** — Continue accepting signatures from old keys (minimum 7 days)
rows.notes: Grace-period acceptance is receiver-side behavior over time.

rows.requirement: Content-Digest follows RFC 9530 and hashes the raw body bytes; implementations MUST use sha-256.
rows.quote:
`Content-Digest` follows [RFC 9530](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9530) and
hashes the raw body bytes. This binds the message body to the signature without
requiring JSON canonicalization. Implementations **MUST** use `sha-256`.

rows.requirement: Proxies, API gateways, and other intermediaries MUST NOT re-serialize JSON bodies, as this would invalidate the signature.
rows.quote:
Proxies, API gateways, and other intermediaries
**MUST NOT** re-serialize JSON bodies, as this would invalidate the signature.
rows.notes: Intermediary behavior; only observable via a verifying receiver.

rows.requirement: Content-Digest header is required when a request/response has a body.
rows.quote:
| `Content-Digest`  | Request/Response | Cond.*   | SHA-256 hash of request/response body |
...
\* Required when request/response has a body
rows.notes: Conditional requirement: body present => Content-Digest required.

rows.requirement: Signature-Input and Signature headers are required on requests and responses.
rows.quote:
| `Signature-Input` | Request/Response | Yes      | Describes signed components           |
| `Signature`       | Request/Response | Yes      | Contains signature value              |

rows.requirement: REST request signed components MUST include @method, @authority, and @path (all Required).
rows.quote:
| `@method`         | Yes       | HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.)          |
| `@authority`      | Yes       | Target host (prevents cross-host relay)|
| `@path`           | Yes       | Request path                           |

rows.requirement: REST request signed components MUST include content-digest and content-type when the request has a body.
rows.quote:
| `content-digest`  | Cond.†    | Body digest (if body present)          |
| `content-type`    | Cond.†    | Content-Type (if body present)         |
...
† Required if request has a body

rows.requirement: idempotency-key MUST be a signed component for POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH requests.
rows.quote:
| `idempotency-key` | Cond.\*** | Idempotency header (state-changing)    |
...
\*** Required for POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH

rows.requirement: @query MUST be a signed component if the request has query parameters.
rows.quote:
| `@query`          | Cond.\*   | Query string (if present)              |
...
\* Required if request has query parameters

rows.requirement: ucp-agent MUST be a signed component if the UCP-Agent header is present.
rows.quote:
| `ucp-agent`       | Cond.\**  | Profile URL (binds identity)           |
...
\** Required if `UCP-Agent` header is present

rows.requirement: ECDSA signatures MUST use fixed-width raw r||s encoding per RFC 9421, not ASN.1/DER (64 bytes P-256, 96 bytes P-384).
rows.quote:
ECDSA signatures **MUST** use fixed-width raw `r||s`
encoding per RFC 9421, **not** ASN.1/DER. The signature value is the
concatenation of `r` and `s` as fixed-length unsigned big-endian integers:
64 bytes for P-256 (32 + 32), 96 bytes for P-384 (48 + 48).
rows.notes: Raw r||s not DER; verification row, suite stubs signature.

rows.requirement: REST response signed components use @status instead of @method (Required).
rows.quote:
Response signatures use `@status` instead of `@method`:
...
| `@status`        | Yes      | HTTP status code (200, 201, etc.) |

rows.requirement: REST response signed components MUST include content-digest and content-type if the response has a body.
rows.quote:
| `content-digest` | Cond.*   | Body digest (if body present)     |
| `content-type`   | Cond.*   | Content-Type (if body present)    |
...
\* Required if response has a body
rows.notes: Response-signing behavior verified by the receiving party.

rows.requirement: Idempotency-Key must have minimum 128 bits of entropy (e.g., UUID v4, 22+ char alphanumeric).
rows.quote: | **Entropy** | Minimum 128 bits (e.g., UUID v4, 22+ char alphanumeric) |

rows.requirement: Server storage of idempotency keys must be a minimum of 24 hours, recommended 48 hours.
rows.quote: | **Server storage** | Minimum 24 hours, recommended 48 hours |

rows.requirement: On idempotency storage failure, fail closed (reject request with 503).
rows.quote: | **On storage failure** | Fail closed (reject request with 503) |

rows.requirement: On duplicate idempotency key, return cached response and do not re-execute.
rows.quote: | **On duplicate** | Return cached response, do not re-execute |

rows.requirement: Platforms SHOULD sign all requests when using HTTP Message Signatures.
rows.quote:
Platforms **SHOULD** sign all requests when using HTTP Message
Signatures.

rows.requirement: Webhook notifications MUST be signed.
rows.quote:
Webhook notifications **MUST** be signed. Recipients cannot
otherwise verify authenticity of server-initiated push messages.
rows.notes: Webhook-signing row; requires a receiver/webhook sender.

rows.requirement: Signatures are RECOMMENDED for payment authorization responses and checkout completion responses.
rows.quote:
Signatures are **RECOMMENDED** for:

* Payment authorization responses
* Checkout completion responses

rows.requirement: Signatures are OPTIONAL for cart operations, catalog queries, and error responses (4xx, 5xx).
rows.quote:
Signatures are **OPTIONAL** for:

* Cart operations (low-value, synchronous)
* Catalog queries (read-only)
* Error responses (4xx, 5xx)

rows.requirement: The RFC 9421 created parameter is OPTIONAL; UCP handles replay protection at the business layer via idempotency keys, not signature timestamps.
rows.quote:
The RFC 9421 `created` parameter is **OPTIONAL**. UCP handles replay
protection at the business layer through idempotency keys, not signature timestamps.

rows.requirement: signature_missing maps to HTTP 401 (required signature header/field not present).
rows.quote: | `signature_missing`     | 401  | Required signature header/field not present          |
rows.notes: Error code/status binding; verified by signature-checking receiver.

rows.requirement: signature_invalid maps to HTTP 401 (signature verification failed).
rows.quote: | `signature_invalid`     | 401  | Signature verification failed                        |

rows.requirement: key_not_found maps to HTTP 401 (key ID not found in signer's signing_keys).
rows.quote: | `key_not_found`         | 401  | Key ID not found in signer's `signing_keys`          |

rows.requirement: digest_mismatch maps to HTTP 400 (body digest doesn't match Content-Digest header).
rows.quote: | `digest_mismatch`       | 400  | Body digest doesn't match `Content-Digest` header    |

rows.requirement: algorithm_unsupported maps to HTTP 400 (signature algorithm not supported).
rows.quote: | `algorithm_unsupported` | 400  | Signature algorithm not supported                    |

rows.requirement: Verification must reconstruct the signature base and reject with signature_invalid if ECDSA verification fails.
rows.quote:
if not ecdsa_verify(signature_base, signature, public_key):
        return error("signature_invalid")
rows.notes: Verification algorithm pseudocode; suite stubs signature so official_oracle=false.

rows.requirement: Verification must return key_not_found when the kid is not present in the signer's signing_keys.
rows.quote:
if not public_key:
        return error("key_not_found")

rows.requirement: Verification must return digest_mismatch when Content-Digest does not match the recomputed sha-256 of the body bytes.
rows.quote:
if request.headers["Content-Digest"] != expected:
            return error("digest_mismatch")

notes: Area is NEW in the 2026-04-08 spec (docs/specification/signatures.md).

OFFICIAL ORACLE: The vendored conformance suite STUBS the request signature. integration_test_utils.get_headers() (.vendor/conformance/integration_test_utils.py:133) sets the header request-signature: "test" — a literal placeholder, not an RFC 9421 Signature/Signature-Input. The suite therefore does NOT actually generate, sign, or verify any signature. Consequently official_oracle=false for ALL signature rows, including verification, error-code, and digest rows.
Note the suite uses a non-standard header name 'request-signature' rather than the spec's RFC 9421 'Signature'/'Signature-Input' headers, further confirming the stub is not a real implementation.

browser_capable=false for all signature verification rows (CORS + WebCrypto ECDSA raw r||s constraints). Only the two pure-doc-structural rows (SIG-007 JWK format, SIG-008 signing_keys publication) are marked browser_capable=true.

Webhook-signing (SIG-027) and receiver-side behaviors (key-rotation grace, idempotency storage/fail-closed, response signing, error-code status bindings) are marked needs-receiver: they require a counterparty that actually signs/verifies, which the stubbed suite does not provide.
Keyword mapping: RECOMMENDED -> SHOULD (SIG-028); OPTIONAL -> MAY (SIG-003, SIG-005, SIG-029, SIG-030). SIG-006 captured as MUST NOT (alg NOT included in Signature-Input).
Replay protection is at the business/idempotency layer, not the signature layer; the created timestamp is OPTIONAL (SIG-030).
